title: Add discover/subscribe as primary command names, keep describe/watch as aliases
description: Both issue #1929 and discussion #306 use 'discover' (not 'describe') and 'subscribe' (not 'watch'). Cobra supports aliases via the Aliases field on cobra.Command. Switch to discover/subscribe as primary names and add describe/watch as backwards-compatible aliases. Update help text, README, skills, and conformance tests accordingly.
close_reason: discover/subscribe now primary; describe/watch kept as aliases via Cobra Aliases field

title: Improve Artifact Retrieval UX: Add 'download/retrieve' command, enable saving from 'status', and add --file flag
Add --wait / --sync flag to 'send' command for blocking execution returning only final results
Restructure CLI commands to align semantics (send, watch, get, chat, cancel, config)
Add --output flag with tui/text/json modes
description:
Add a --output <mode> global flag supporting three output modes: 'tui' (default, interactive Bubble Tea), 'text' (non-interactive human-readable, no animations, for CI/piped output), 'json' (machine-readable NDJSON, equivalent to current -n/--no-tui).

Design decisions:
- Long form only (--output), no -o shorthand — -o is already taken by --out-dir on send/get/watch/download
- Keep -n/--no-tui as a backward-compatible alias for --output json (do not remove)
- NO_COLOR=true and A2ACLI_NO_TUI=true should map to --output text rather than --output json (plain human-readable, not machine-readable)
- 'text' mode is the non-interactive human experience: formatted output, no spinners/animations — equivalent to what git does when stdout is not a terminal

Aligns with the --output/-o flag proposed in a2aproject/A2A#1929 and a2aproject/a2a-go discussion #306. The -o shorthand conflict is a known gap vs those proposals.

title: Add send --immediate flag (fire-and-forget)
description: Both issue #1929 (--return-immediately) and discussion #306 (--immediate) specify a fire-and-forget mode for send: set ReturnImmediately in SendMessageConfig and return the Task ID immediately without waiting for completion or streaming. The user can then use 'get' or 'watch' to follow up. Currently a2acli only supports blocking (--wait) or streaming (default).
close_reason: Added --immediate flag to send; sets ReturnImmediately:true and returns task ID immediately

title: Add --timeout global flag
description: Discussion #306 specifies a global --timeout <dur> flag with a default of 30s. Currently a2acli has no request timeout, which means hung agents block indefinitely. Add as a persistent flag on rootCmd, apply to all HTTP/gRPC client calls. Should accept Go duration strings (e.g. 30s, 2m).

title: Implement serve --exec mode
description: Discussion #306 specifies an exec mode for 'serve': wrap any subprocess as an A2A agent. Message text goes to stdin, stdout becomes the response artifact. Exit code 0 = TASK_STATE_COMPLETED, non-zero = TASK_STATE_FAILED. Supports a --chunk=<delimiter> flag for streaming output incrementally as ArtifactUpdateEvents. The flag exists in code but is marked 'not yet supported'.

title: Implement serve --proxy mode
description: Discussion #306 specifies a proxy mode for 'serve': forward all requests to an upstream A2A agent, log traffic to stderr, inject service parameters and auth headers. The flag exists in code but is marked 'not yet supported'. Implementation should create an a2aclient.Client for the upstream, forward each A2A operation, inject --svc-param values via AttachServiceParams, and derive the proxy's own AgentCard from the upstream card with the local address substituted.

title: Update a2a-go SDK to latest version and fix tests
Refactor colorizedHelpFunc to use semantic tokens instead of hardcoded ANSI/lipgloss colors
Implement adaptive/theme-aware coloring for optimal contrast in both Light and Dark terminal modes
Audit a2acli help and TUI output to maximize data-ink ratio (remove non-essential color/borders)
Introduce semantic color tokens (Accent, Muted, Pass, Warn, Fail) for consistent styling
Audit and synchronize flag short-names across all commands
Standardize 'Hint' guidance for common error states (missing config, invalid IDs)
Implement basic semantic help styling (colorized headers and flags)
Add 'Long' descriptions and 'Example' blocks to all core commands
Organize a2acli commands into logical groups (Tasks, Info, System)
Update README.md documenting how --wait/--sync maps to A2A SendMessage Blocking:true parameter
Update README.md with new CLI command semantics (send, watch, get)
Implement 'cancel' command to terminate tasks
Configuration Management (XDG + Named Server Environments, --config)
Interactive Chat Mode REPL using Bubbletea
Phase 3: Migrate a2acli to use the a2a-go SDK release/spec-v1 branch
Phase 2: Add TCK SUT to Makefile and implement baseline conformance tests using os/exec
Phase 1: Add non-interactive mode (--no-tui) and JSON output for CLI testability
Add golangci-lint configured to align with Google Go standards
In addition to .env config, let's use XDG base directory configs
Add structured JSON message input to send (--parts, --json)
description: Discussion #306 specifies two flags for non-text message input: --json <body> accepts a raw JSON Message object, --parts <json> accepts a raw JSON parts array (CLI wraps it in a ROLE_USER Message). Also -f <file> to read a Message from a JSON file. Currently a2acli only accepts plain text as a positional argument, limiting use with multi-modal agents (file URLs, data parts, etc.).

title: Add list tasks filters: --context, --status, --since, --with-artifacts
description: Discussion #306 specifies richer filtering for 'list tasks': --context <id> (filter by context ID), --status <state> (filter by task state, short forms: submitted/working/completed/failed/canceled/rejected), --since <time> (RFC 3339, only tasks updated after this timestamp), --with-artifacts (include artifacts in response), --history <n> (include history messages per task). Currently a2acli only supports --limit and --page-token.

title: Expose A2A CLI as an MCP server via STDIO (--mcp)
Support dynamic transport selection (gRPC vs JSONRPC) based on AgentCard SupportedInterfaces
